commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a 300s active-reply wait for visible dispatch admission and a Telegram forum-topic regression test covering that wait.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests +46. Total +54 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Source inspection shows the current dispatch path uses the 15s registry default for active visible turns, but I did not establish a live Telegram failing path.
commands.review_followup: Manual review is needed for Telegram proof and acceptance of the 300s wait tradeoff; there is no narrow automated code repair to request now.

target.title: fix(reply): queue visible turns behind active replies
target.body:
## Summary
- Keep visible reply dispatches waiting up to 300s behind an active same-session reply operation instead of falling through the 15s `reply-operation-active` skip path.
- Leave heartbeat admission behavior unchanged so background/context-engine turns still skip while a visible reply owns the lane.
- Add a Telegram forum-topic regression for message `2444` shape that verifies visible dispatch uses a bounded 300s active-run wait and reaches the reply resolver.

## Verification
- `node --import tsx - <<'EOF' ... runtime admission probe ... EOF`
- `CI=1 OPENCLAW_TEST_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts --pool=threads --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot -t "Telegram topic"`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`

## Real Behavior Proof
Behavior addressed: A visible Telegram forum-topic user message arriving while a same-session heartbeat/background reply operation is active should wait for the active operation to clear instead of being skipped as `reply-operation-active` after the default 15s admission timeout. The wait is intentionally bounded to 300s so leaked active operations recover as `reply-operation-active` instead of leaving the visible turn pending indefinitely.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout with the patched source loaded through Node/tsx, using the real reply-run registry and reply-turn admission modules against the same Telegram forum-topic session-key shape from topic 547.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx` runtime probe created an active reply operation for `agent:main:telegram:group:-1003774691294:topic:3731`, completed it after 50ms, then admitted a visible turn with `waitTimeoutMs: 300_000`.

Evidence after fix: Console output from the local OpenClaw runtime probe:

```text
runtime-probe: visible admission status=owned elapsedMs=51
runtime-probe: adoptedSessionId=active-session
runtime-probe: activeAfter=false
```

Observed result after fix: The visible Telegram-topic turn waited for the active operation, adopted the active session id, and left no active reply operation behind after completion. The focused Telegram-topic dispatch tests also passed and confirmed heartbeat turns still skip while the lane is active.

What was not tested: I did not run a live Telegram topic message through a deployed gateway; the after-fix runtime probe and focused dispatch/admission tests cover the queue/admission behavior locally.
